batches.batch_rationale: T1 is the foundational prerequisite — dirty-state detection must exist before any navigation intercept or dialog can reference it. Must execute alone and complete before Batch 2.

uows.title: Dirty State Detection
uows.rationale: No dependencies; foundational signal that T2, T3, and T4 all depend on. Highest-risk task due to unknown existing codebase patterns — scheduled first to fail fast.
definition_of_done: Dirty-state detection mechanism identified (reuse existing) or created (new service/flag)
Signal set correctly when page content is modified
Signal cleared correctly after successful save
Jest unit tests cover set/clear behavior for the dirty-state mechanism

batches.batch_rationale: T2 (guard/interceptor logic) and T3 (dialog component) both depend only on T1 and have no shared file or state dependency. T2 operates on route/menu infrastructure; T3 operates on a standalone UI component. Safe to parallelize — no merge conflict risk.

uows.title: Menu Navigation Interceptor
uows.rationale: Depends on T1. Implements guard/interceptor logic against router or menu click handlers — pure logic layer, no UI template dependency.
definition_of_done: Menu navigation is intercepted when dirty state is true
Menu navigation proceeds unimpeded when dirty state is false
Intercept strategy matches the menu's underlying navigation mechanism (router vs. handler)
Jest unit tests cover guard logic: dirty true blocks, dirty false allows

uows.title: Unsaved Changes Dialog Component
uows.rationale: Depends on T1. Implements or reuses the dialog UI component — separate concern from interceptor logic, distinct file set, can proceed concurrently with T2.
definition_of_done: Dialog renders with Save & Continue, Discard & Continue, and Cancel actions
Dialog integrates with application design system (no bespoke styling divergence)
Dialog exposes an async API that resolves to the user's chosen action
Cypress component test written covering all three action outcomes
Test harness created/updated with data-test-id selectors for all three buttons
nx component-test passes with no failures

batches.batch_rationale: T4 integrates all prior outputs (T2 interceptor + T3 dialog) into the end-to-end flow. Cannot begin until both UOW-002 and UOW-003 are complete.

uows.title: Integration — Wire Guard, Dialog, and Save/Discard Outcomes
uows.rationale: Depends on both T2 and T3. Wires all three layers together and validates the complete end-to-end navigation-guard flow including all three dialog outcomes.
definition_of_done: Save & Continue path: save is triggered, navigation proceeds on success, stays on error
Discard & Continue path: dirty state cleared, navigation proceeds
Cancel path: dialog closed, user remains on page, no navigation occurs
Feature is manually smoke-tested via menu navigation with and without unsaved changes
All existing route guard or navigation tests pass without regression

risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Unknown codebase patterns: dirty-state mechanism, existing guards, menu implementation technology all unresolved. Foundational to all other UoWs.
Intercept strategy (CanDeactivate guard vs. click handler) is unknown; wrong choice could require rework of T4 wiring.
Existing reusable dialog component may or may not exist; discovery step affects implementation scope.
Integration complexity with save-failure edge case; risk is lower because prior UoWs de-risk the unknowns.

batch_2.safety_rationale: UOW-002 operates on routing/guard infrastructure files; UOW-003 operates on a dialog UI component. No shared file modifications expected. No interdependent logic — both only consume the dirty-state signal from UOW-001, not each other's outputs. Low merge conflict risk.

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: Three-batch sequential-parallel-sequential schedule chosen to respect the natural dependency chain: dirty-state first, then guard+dialog in parallel (separate concerns on distinct file sets), then integration last. The parallel batch recovers time on a 3-point story without introducing merge risk.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Fully sequential execution of all four UoWs
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: T2 and T3 have no mutual dependency and touch separate file sets; sequential execution wastes time with no correctness benefit.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Parallelize T1 with T2 or T3
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: T2 and T3 both require the dirty-state signal established by T1; parallelizing them with T1 would produce integration failures.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Single batch for all four UoWs
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Dependency violations: T2, T3, T4 cannot start before T1; T4 cannot start before T2 and T3.

knowledge_gaps: Menu implementation technology (Angular Router vs. custom click handlers) unknown — impacts T2 intercept strategy
Whether existing CanDeactivate guards are present in mcs-products-mono-ui is unknown
Whether an existing unsaved-changes dialog component exists is unknown
Dirty-state detection mechanism currently used in Specimen Accessioning pages is unknown
